name: ISO/IEC 38500:2015
description: International standard for governance of IT providing guiding principles for directors of organizations on the effective, efficient, and acceptable use of IT within their organizations. Establishes six governance principles and an Evaluate-Direct-Monitor (EDM) model for IT governance.
license_note: Metadata only. No ISO copyrighted text reproduced. Descriptions are original summaries.

items.title: Principle 1: Responsibility
items.description: Individuals and groups within the organization understand and accept their responsibilities in respect of both supply of, and demand for, IT. Those with responsibility for actions also have the authority to perform those actions.
items.purpose: Ensure clear responsibility and authority for IT governance decisions.

items.title: Principle 2: Strategy
items.description: The organization's business strategy takes into account current and future capabilities of IT, and the IT strategic plans satisfy the current and ongoing needs of the organization's business strategy.
items.purpose: Ensure IT strategy aligns with and supports business strategy.

items.title: Principle 3: Acquisition
items.description: IT acquisitions are made for valid reasons on the basis of appropriate and ongoing analysis, with clear and transparent decision making. There is appropriate balance between benefits, opportunities, costs, and risks both in the short and long term.
items.purpose: Ensure IT acquisitions are justified, transparent, and balanced.

items.title: Principle 4: Performance
items.description: IT is fit for purpose in supporting the organization, providing the services, levels of service, and service quality required to meet current and future business requirements.
items.purpose: Ensure IT performance supports current and future business requirements.

items.title: Principle 5: Conformance
items.description: IT complies with all mandatory legislation and regulations. Policies and practices are clearly defined, implemented, and enforced in respect of conformance requirements.
items.purpose: Ensure IT complies with all applicable legal, regulatory, and policy requirements.

items.title: Principle 6: Human behaviour
items.description: IT policies, practices, and decisions demonstrate respect for human behaviour, including the current and evolving needs of all people in the process. Considers dignity, privacy, and fair treatment of individuals.
items.purpose: Ensure IT governance respects human needs, dignity, and behaviour.

items.title: Evaluate - Current and future use of IT
items.description: Directors should examine and make judgement on the current and future use of IT, including strategies, proposals, and supply arrangements (internal, external, or both).
items.purpose: Evaluate current and future IT use to inform governance decisions.

items.title: Evaluate - Business processes
items.description: Directors should evaluate the extent to which IT supports current and future needs of the business, including how well IT-enabled business processes contribute to organizational objectives.
items.purpose: Evaluate IT's support for business processes and organizational objectives.

items.title: Evaluate - Risk management
items.description: Directors should evaluate the risks to the business from IT, including cyber security risks, regulatory risks, operational risks, and reputational risks associated with IT use.
items.purpose: Evaluate IT-related risks to the organization.

items.title: Evaluate - Conformance obligations
items.description: Directors should evaluate the conformance obligations of the organization in relation to its use of IT, including contractual, legal, regulatory, and internal policy obligations.
items.purpose: Evaluate conformance obligations related to IT use.

items.title: Direct - IT plans and policies
items.description: Directors should direct the preparation and implementation of plans and policies that ensure the organization benefits from IT use. Plans should align with strategic direction and resource availability.
items.purpose: Direct the preparation and implementation of IT plans and policies.

items.title: Direct - IT resource allocation
items.description: Directors should direct the allocation of appropriate resources to ensure IT capabilities meet current and future organizational needs, balancing investment between maintaining existing systems and developing new capabilities.
items.purpose: Direct appropriate allocation of IT resources to meet organizational needs.

items.title: Direct - IT risk management
items.description: Directors should direct that risk management practices are embedded in the organization's use of IT, establishing risk appetite and ensuring appropriate controls are in place.
items.purpose: Direct the implementation of IT risk management practices.

items.title: Direct - IT conformance
items.description: Directors should direct that policies establishing conformance requirements for IT use are developed and enforced. Covers legal, regulatory, ethical, and internal policy conformance.
items.purpose: Direct the establishment and enforcement of IT conformance policies.

items.title: Direct - Human factors
items.description: Directors should direct that IT use is considerate of human factors, including the establishment of practices and policies that respect the needs, skills, and capabilities of people affected by IT decisions.
items.purpose: Direct that IT use considers human factors and respects people.

items.title: Monitor - IT performance
items.description: Directors should monitor the performance of IT through appropriate measurement systems. Monitoring should provide assurance that performance aligns with business plans, including how well IT supports business processes.
items.purpose: Monitor IT performance against plans and business requirements.

items.title: Monitor - IT compliance
items.description: Directors should monitor the conformance of IT with internal policies, external obligations, and the principles set out in this standard. Covers legal compliance, regulatory compliance, and policy adherence.
items.purpose: Monitor IT conformance with internal and external obligations.

items.title: Monitor - IT risk
items.description: Directors should monitor the effectiveness of risk management practices related to IT use, ensuring that risks remain within acceptable tolerance levels and that emerging risks are identified promptly.
items.purpose: Monitor IT risk management effectiveness and emerging risks.

items.title: Monitor - Resource utilization
items.description: Directors should monitor the use of resources allocated to IT, ensuring they are being utilized efficiently and effectively in support of business objectives.
items.purpose: Monitor IT resource utilization for efficiency and effectiveness.

items.title: Monitor - Business value from IT
items.description: Directors should monitor the overall value being generated from IT investment, including whether planned benefits are being realized and whether lessons learned are being captured and applied.
items.purpose: Monitor business value realization from IT investments.

items.title: Governance structures and processes
items.description: The organization should establish governance structures (such as committees, boards, and working groups) and define processes through which IT governance is exercised. Clear escalation paths and decision-making authorities should be defined.
items.purpose: Establish the organizational structures for IT governance.

items.title: Communication and reporting
items.description: The organization should establish communication and reporting mechanisms that provide directors with timely, relevant, and accurate information about IT performance, risk, and conformance to support governance decision-making.
items.purpose: Establish communication and reporting mechanisms for IT governance.

items.title: IT governance roles
items.description: The organization should define IT governance roles including the board of directors, IT steering committee, CIO, IT management, and business management, with clear responsibilities for each role in the governance model.
items.purpose: Define and assign IT governance roles with clear responsibilities.

items.title: IT governance assessment and audit
items.description: The organization should regularly assess the effectiveness of its IT governance arrangements through self-assessment, internal audit, or external review. Assessment results should inform improvements to governance practices.
items.purpose: Assess the effectiveness of IT governance through regular evaluation.

items.title: IT governance culture
items.description: The organization should foster a culture where IT governance is understood and valued at all levels. This includes awareness of governance principles, training on governance responsibilities, and embedding governance into organizational routines.
items.purpose: Foster an organizational culture that values and practices IT governance.
